from there.

com or a Zillow.

com or Zillow expenses.
So, in terms of how we work, we fully integrate with… your CRM, and property management software.
So, you're working with Resman, and, Lester had mentioned you guys use NOC, but you're transitioning to Elise AI.

We integrate with all of those tools, so anytime you guys update pricing, availability, pictures, you name it, it'll pull and it'll reflect on our rentable site and all of our networks, and then when we deliver leads, we'll deliver them into your Elise AI, or the properties, if they still have NOC, we can deliver there as well.
So, with what we offer, when we advertise your properties, we're gonna advertise them on our site, and then from there, we're gonna syndicate them out to all of our network.
Our syndicate network is… is the largest in the industry.
We're going literally everywhere except for Apartments.
com.

So, we're using AI as well, because I know you guys are using, like, ApartmentList, and then some of your properties have Zillow, so… our AI tool is smart enough to realize that and say, okay, cool, we're not gonna push and duplicate listings there, but in your case, like, let's say we're doing portfolio-wide, if you have 5 of your properties still on Zillow, we won't push and duplicate for those properties, but for the remaining 7 that aren't utilizing it, we can still push and get you over there for those.
Should be on Realtor.
com now.

The other thing that we include is all-inclusive targeted social ads.
So, we're getting your properties over on Facebook and Instagram, and essentially reaching out to renters versus waiting for them to come to you.

So, the way that works is anytime somebody goes on a search engine and says, show me apartments in Dallas, Texas, or in Farmer's Branch, the next time they get on their Facebook or Instagram feeds, within about 30 to 45 seconds of scrolling, we'll hit them with a targeted runnable ad that looks similar to this.
It'll have pictures of your individual property, it'll have some details about it as well.
We advertise things like lowest advertised price, if there's, like, pet friendly, if there's an in-unit washer-dryer, a pool, whatever it may be.
Some details about the property just to help it stand out and attract that individual renter.
And then, from there, the way our ads differ from a lot of other companies is a lot of other companies usually do one of two things.
They either redirect into their personal site, so then at that point, a renter can see all the properties they're actually doing ads for.

Or, they'll redirect them to the property website, which is awesome, but at the end of the day, you don't know if that person ends up becoming a lead, you don't know if they… you don't really know what happens from there, you kind of lose track of it.
With all of our ads, they're lead generating.

So, when somebody clicks Learn More, we're gonna have them fill out a form that captures their name, their email, and their phone number, and then we're gonna deliver that to you as a guest card directly into your Knocker or Elise AI.
And that's how it works.
Go ahead.
Is there a cap on the ads?
Because I'm assuming it's a paid ad.
It is a paid ad.
what would be RCAP, monthly.
So… so the way we do everything, we do… it's, like, typically 3 different ads a week.
And then we rotate everything around, so we don't necessarily cap you on leads here.
The reason for that is we've had a back-end channel partnership with Facebook and Meta for years, so we're getting all of these ads for, like, pennies to the dollar.
Compared to, like, somebody such as yourself, if you individually went in and did these.

We do, but it also goes based off of, like, their search, right?
So, if somebody is, let's say, looking in Houston for properties in Houston, they're not going to pull up properties in Dallas.

And then, if they're looking for specific things, like, let's say they're saying 2 bedrooms, then if this unit doesn't have any 2 bedrooms available, then it's probably not going to pull for that individual.

If they're searching things, like, if… that there's a pool, but the community doesn't have a pool, which nowadays I'd say 95% do, but just for an example, like, it probably wouldn't pull that property.
So, yes, we do advertising for other companies as well that may pull up, but… It usually goes based off of the individual search, and… Surrounding area.

down on the actual results that are… that you're wanting.

our AI tools also learning what pictures are getting the better engagement?

this AI tool will understand and see where you're getting more impressions, so if you're getting more impressions on the playground or the pool compared to the kitchen, it'll move those closer to the front and have them be first more often.
